understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting all selectable pieces onto the goal tiles marked with a '+' at the same time (i.e., every goal must be occupied simultaneously for the engine to report WIN), within the limited step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is a currently selected piece. A CLICK on the grid attempts to move the selected piece; if the click corresponds to a valid movement direction/line (e.g., along the same row/column) the piece slides in that direction until it hits an obstacle/boundary and stops at the last free cell before the block. If the click does not define a valid move (not aligned) or the path is immediately blocked, the piece does not move and the action is still consumed. Walls/obstacles act as hard blockers; attempted moves into/through them fail. I did not observe any successful pushing of other pieces; blocked results suggest pieces cannot pass through blockers and may not be able to push at all (or pushing requires a different setup that never occurred).

orient_history: Movement keys try to move the currently selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell), but walls can fully lock a piece. Likely we can reposition/select pieces by clicking on another selectable or on/near the goal tile to place/teleport the selected piece.
CLICK selects a different selectable piece; movement applies to the currently selected piece, and pieces cannot move through collidable walls/objects.

CLICK likely either (a) selects a piece if you click on it, or (b) attempts to move the currently selected piece to the clicked location when allowed. Since the currently selected piece is trapped, we should switch selection to the other piece and try moving that one onto the goal region.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* attempts a 1-cell (3px) step and respects wall collisions. CLICK on another selectable swaps the selected piece’s position with that other selectable (ignoring intervening walls), potentially letting us reach a position with different available moves toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK is primarily for selecting a piece or targeting a destination; the earlier goal click may have failed due to clicking at an edge/invalid point. Since MOVE is blocked, the only plausible progress is via CLICK-based repositioning/teleporting onto the goal area if allowed.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is active (selection jumps between the two pieces) rather than freely moving through walls; need to select the other piece to check if it has any available movement/interaction toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK can interact with objects (possibly toggling/removing a wall or opening a passage). Since normal movement is fully blocked for both selectables, we likely must change the wall layout (e.g., click a specific wall segment) to create a path to the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK on another selectable swaps/teleports the currently-controlled piece to that selectable’s position (effectively selecting the other piece). After selecting the right-hand selectable, a subsequent CLICK on the goal may allow moving that piece onto the goal (even though arrow-moves are blocked).

Hypothesis: CLICK changes selection when clicking a selectable; clicking non-selectable tiles may interact (e.g., toggle/remove) special wall/door tiles. Since direct movement is blocked, progress likely requires interacting with a wall segment (possibly a door) near the barrier between pieces and the goal region.

CLICK appears to trigger a jump/swap-like move between the two selectable positions (likely by clicking on/near the other selectable). Arrow-key moves are standard grid moves but currently impossible because the selected piece is enclosed by walls. Next step is to switch the selected piece back to the other position to test if that position has different mobility or enables further interactions.

Hypothesis: CLICK on another selectable swaps/changes which piece is active; MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece by one 3-pixel cell if not blocked. Since the current selected is immobile, we likely need to select the other piece to find a movable corridor toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a piece (and may appear like the 'selected piece moved' due to selection switching). MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by one cell (3 px) but is prevented by collidable walls. Need to discover a mechanic to reach the goal despite apparent enclosure (e.g., hidden breakable wall, special interaction not yet triggered).

Hypothesis: CLICK on/near another selectable switches selection to that piece (so the reported 'selected piece moved' is actually selection focus jumping). MOVE_* moves only the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) if not blocked.

Hypothesis: CLICK can switch which selectable is controlled (by clicking on/near another selectable) and may also trigger special repositioning/jumping that bypasses the normal MOVE_* blocking. To make progress toward the goal area, I should first select the other piece at [21,21] and then test its available movement/jump behavior.

CLICK on a selectable likely switches which piece is selected (it appears as the 'player' moving to that selectable). Movement is grid-step based (3 pixels per step). Since the current piece is immobile, we should switch to the other selectable and try moving it toward the goal region or to a position that enables progress.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* only works when adjacent space is open; CLICK attempts to relocate the selected piece to the clicked tile/region if it is a valid destination (not wall/blocked), possibly allowing teleport across compartments (e.g., to the goal region). Need to get selectable(s) onto the goal area to trigger WIN.

CLICK seems to reposition the selected piece to specific reachable pockets (not necessarily the clicked tile), likely to the nearest open space/anchor on that side of a wall. From the left pocket at [9,21], the main way to make progress is to CLICK to the middle/right pocket around [18,21] and then use MOVE steps to navigate toward the goal around the large wall at x≈24–29.

CLICK seems to change which selectable is controlled when clicking on/near the other piece; MOVE_* then moves the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 px) when not blocked by walls. To reach the goal area, we should try controlling the right-hand selectable at [21,21] and probe available moves/click-transfers from there.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is currently controlled (or attempts context-based movement); arrow moves shift by one cell (3 px) but are blocked by collidable walls. Current selected piece is stuck, so we should select the other piece at [9,21] to probe available moves toward the goal area.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* only works within open pockets but we are currently enclosed; CLICK can relocate the selected piece to a clicked cell/pocket (possibly within the same row), even across walls. Prior failed click near the goal may have been due to wrong row (y=6 instead of y=7).

CLICK likely interacts with selectables differently than MOVE: clicking on/near another selectable may swap positions or change which piece is selected. To make progress, we probably need to control the other selectable at [21,21] and see if it has a path toward the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* moves the currently selected 1-cell piece by 1 cell (3 px) but is blocked by collidable walls. CLICK usually selects a piece if clicked on it; however, some wall tiles may be interactive (removable/toggle), which would be required since all open spaces are isolated in the semantic grid.

CLICK on a selectable switches which piece is selected (it looks like a 'move' in the log because the controlled/selected piece changes). Actual movement likely requires selecting the piece that has available space and then using MOVE_* to navigate toward the goal region.

CLICK appears to select the object under the cursor (the 'player' becomes that selectable), which previously looked like 'movement' between [9,21] and [21,21]. To make progress we likely need to select the other piece and see if it has any available movement/path toward the goal area.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* cannot work because the selected piece is fully adjacent-blocked; progress likely requires CLICKing an interactive object (possibly the goal itself, at its exact pixel position) to teleport/place the selected selectable onto the goal region.

CLICK likely either (a) switches selection when clicking on a selectable, or (b) attempts to move/teleport the currently selected piece to the clicked location if valid. The goal seems to occupy multiple semantic cells; previous goal clicks may have missed the effective clickable/center pixel for a valid placement.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches selection; CLICK on certain empty/goal tiles may move/teleport the currently selected piece if the click lands within the valid goal area (not on a wall/edge pixel).

CLICK likely changes selection when clicking on another selectable; MOVE_* moves the selected piece by 1 cell (3px) but collisions block. If stuck, a plausible missing mechanic is that some walls/doors may toggle via CLICK on the wall region (wall_24_12).

CLICKing on the other selectable likely switches control / swaps the active piece to that other position; CLICKing non-selectable targets (like the goal) only works if the currently active piece is allowed to move/teleport there under the click rules. Since MOVE is impossible from [21,21], we should first switch to the other selectable and then attempt a goal-click.

Hypothesis: CLICK moves/teleports the selected piece to the clicked cell only if the click lands on a valid anchor cell (aligned to the 3-pixel grid). The goal region likely contains at least one such anchor (e.g., its top-left aligned cell near [33,15]). We need to place a selectable onto the goal region to trigger WIN.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to reposition/teleport the currently selected 3x3 selectable to the clicked pixel location if it can fit there without colliding with walls; previous failures may have been due to slight misalignment, so aim for a grid-aligned pixel inside the goal area (multiple of 3).

Clicks appear to switch which selectable is active (reported as the selected piece 'moving' between the two selectable positions). Movement is grid-based (3px per cell) but current position is fully enclosed by walls. With 0 steps remaining, the engine likely will not allow any further state-changing actions, so the level cannot be completed from this state.

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting all selectable pieces onto the goal tiles marked with a '+' at the same time (i.e., every goal must be occupied simultaneously for the engine to report WIN), within the limited step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is a currently selected piece. A CLICK on the grid attempts to move the selected piece; if the click corresponds to a valid movement direction/line (e.g., along the same row/column) the piece slides in that direction until it hits an obstacle/boundary and stops at the last free cell before the block. If the click does not define a valid move (not aligned) or the path is immediately blocked, the piece does not move and the action is still consumed. Walls/obstacles act as hard blockers; attempted moves into/through them fail. I did not observe any successful pushing of other pieces; blocked results suggest pieces cannot pass through blockers and may not be able to push at all (or pushing requires a different setup that never occurred).
